summary: Implement M3 minimal succession state machine
acceptance_criteria: A minimal deterministic succession state machine represents ruler death/incapacity, candidate set formation, support dimensions, pending resolution, and at least peaceful succession plus disputed/regency or split outcome.

Character death or removal creates office vacancies, prevents dead characters from proposing, commanding, holding new offices, or becoming new relationship endpoints, and keeps jurisdiction policies intact where applicable.
Candidate support sources are explicit and serializable, including kinship/designation/court/military/provincial/suzerain or foreign support as M3-scoped abstractions.
Succession state transitions are driven by sim-core events/commands and use stable tie-breakers; no random, real-time, UI, or object graph authority is introduced.
Scenario tests cover peaceful transition, vacancy generation, invalid dead-character actions, and one disputed or regency-style branch.
No full civil war, full diplomacy, event narrative system, formal historical dynasty database, client UI, or M4+ war behavior is introduced.
required_tests: Property/state-machine tests cover valid succession transitions, dead-character invariants, office vacancy handling, policy persistence, and deterministic candidate tie-breakers.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-SUCCESSION-STATE-MACHINE-001
notes: This is the Roadmap M3 minimal state machine only; do not build M6 full succession/civil-war systems.
If historical succession rules are needed for real polities, route to historical_researcher instead of inventing them.
Keep support dimensions inspectable so later UI can display reasons without duplicating formulas.
